title: Core Theorems v2.1 - Reference
content:
# Core Theorems v2.1 - Reference

Overview
Core mathematical theorems for the scbe-aethermoore package, version 2.1.
Quick Installation
npm i scbe-aethermoore
Repository: github.com/issdandavis/scbe-aethermoore-demo
Documentation: README
The Four Core Theorems
Theorem 1: Polar Decomposition Uniqueness
Every bounded linear operator on a Hilbert space has a unique polar decomposition into a partial isometry and a positive operator.
Key Properties:
Uniqueness of decomposition
Partial isometry structure
Positive operator guarantees
Theorem 2: Isometric Realification
Complex inner product spaces can be isometrically embedded into real vector spaces while preserving geometric structure.
Key Properties:
Isometric embedding
Geometric structure preservation
Real-complex space bridging
Theorem 3: Poincaré Ball Containment
The Poincaré ball model provides a bounded representation of hyperbolic space with well-defined containment properties.
Key Properties:
Bounded hyperbolic representation
Distance metric preservation
Containment axioms
Theorem 4: Hyperbolic Metric Axioms
The hyperbolic metric satisfies all metric space axioms and provides a foundation for non-Euclidean geometry.
Key Properties:
Metric space axioms
Non-Euclidean foundations
Distance function properties
Interactive Test Application
Test and visualize all four theorems with the interactive demo app included in the package.
Version: 2.1
Last Updated: February 4, 2026
Package Information
NPM Package: scbe-aethermoore
Version: 2.1
GitHub: issdandavis/scbe-aethermoore-demo
License: Check repository for details
Maintained by: Issac Davis
Usage
After installation, import the theorems:
import { PolarDecomposition, IsometricRealification, PoincareBall, HyperbolicMetric } from 'scbe-aethermoore';
See the repository documentation for detailed API references and examples.
SCBE-AETHERMOORE System Architecture
1. Core Axioms (Foundational Principles)
These are the irreducible truths the entire framework rests on:
Geometric Containment Axiom: All agent state, reasoning, observations, and actions MUST reside within bounded hyperbolic space (Poincaré ball). Unsafe drifts exponentially approach the boundary (event horizon at r > 1.0), enabling hard, provable rejection.
Aperiodic Resilience Axiom: Reasoning uses quasicrystal lattices (non-periodic, φ-based) to resist pattern exploitation, hallucinations, and adversarial drifts—topological invariants detect deviations.
Harmonic Truth Axiom: Verification occurs via spectral/symphonic coherence (φ-weighted frequencies in Six Sacred Tongues); dissonance triggers immediate containment.
Post-Quantum Sovereignty Axiom: All communication, signatures, and secrets use PQC primitives; extended to novel quasicrystal-based hardness.
Multi-Agent Mesh Axiom: Agents coordinate via trusted geometric cones; Byzantine faults contained by quorum overlap in 6D hyperbolic vectors.
Closed-Loop Safety Axiom: Vision → Reasoning → Action → Observation forms a unified manifold in hyperbolic space—no flat embeddings allowed.
These axioms ensure superiority over heuristic sandboxes (Anthropic/OpenAI agents) by baking containment into representation geometry.
2. Geometric Foundations
Hyperbolic Space
Poincaré ball model (curvature c=1.0 default, dim=8–16).
Exponential volume growth: Fine control near origin (safe actions), rapid exclusion near boundary
Operations: Möbius addition, exponential/log maps, geodesic distances
Memory Rings (Concentric radii)
<table>
14-Layer Hyperbolic Governance
H(d,R) = R^(d²) scaling. Layer 12 enforces "Harmonic Wall" (spectral fingerprinting + Langues weighting: W_total = Σ w_i × e^(φ×i)).
3. PHDM Quasicrystal Brain (Core Reasoning Engine)
Structure
16 polyhedral cognitive zones in quasicrystal lattice (icosahedral symmetry, φ-proportioned).
Projection: Higher-D periodic → 3D/6D aperiodic via cut-and-project
"Thoughts" = Hamiltonian paths through zones
Mechanisms
Phason shifting: Adaptive flips for resilience (secret parameter in crypto extensions)
Topological CFI: Persistent homology/Betti numbers detect hallucinations (99% claimed)
Simplified entry point: Poincaré radius/angular checks (initial implementation)
Evolution Path
Simplified (radius checks only)
Graph zones (8–10 polyhedra)
Full 16-zone Hamiltonian + phason
4. Security & Crypto Layer
Existing PQC
Kyber768 (KEM) + Dilithium3 (signatures)
Symphonic Cipher: Six Sacred Tongues (typed communication) with φ-based frequencies
Verification
Real-time: FFT spectral fingerprinting on actions
Quasicrystal signatures for trust
New Primitive: QuasiLWE
Hard problem: Approximate SVP/CVP in aperiodic quasicrystal lattice.
Key idea: Matrix A from φ-irrational projections (no translational symmetry)
Phason secret: Optional hidden shift for decryption advantage
Extensions planned: Full KEM, hash, PRNG via polyhedral paths
QuasiLWE advantages over standard LWE:
Lacks periodic structure exploits
Natural hallucination/attack detection via crystallinity checks
Adaptive resilience through phason shifts
Ties directly to reasoning geometry
5. Browser Agent Capabilities (Vision-Action Loop)
Core Loop
Observe (Playwright screenshot/DOM → CLIP/SigLIP)
Embed (Vision → hyperbolic projection head)
Reason (PHDM path in lattice)
Validate (Radius + curvature + topological checks)
Act (Only low-radius geodesics executed)
Components (Directory: agents/browser/)
phdm_brain.py: Simplified PHDM class (Poincaré ops, is_safe())
playwright_wrapper.py: Headless Chrome control with timeouts
vision_embedding.py: CLIP → Poincaré linear/head
main.py: FastAPI endpoint orchestrating loop
Superior Features
Hard containment (no heuristic RL guards)
Hallucination detection via curvature jumps
Unified manifold (vision/reasoning/action in same space)
6. Multi-Agent Coordination (Spiralverse Protocol)
Communication
Six Sacred Tongues + quasicrystal signatures
Navigation: 6D vectors (X/Y/Z + Time + Priority + Confidence)
Trust
Hyperbolic cones (angular width ∝ trust score)
Fault Tolerance: N ≥ 3f + 1 Byzantine, enforced by geometric state overlap
Mesh
Agent-to-agent direct (planned swarm simulation)
QuasiLWE + Spiralverse Integration
Native Geometric Security: 6D vectors encrypted directly in quasicrystal lattice
Dynamic Keys: Phason parameters as shared secrets for session rotation
Byzantine resistance: Aperiodic structure prevents collusion patterns
Containment enforcement: Decryption fails if ciphertext pushes toward event horizon
7. Deployment & Infrastructure
Multi-Cloud
AWS Lambda: Stateless execution, hyperbolic validation (deploy-aws.yml CI/CD ready)
GCP Cloud Run: Session state persistence (hyperbolic vectors)
Docker
Playwright + Chrome base (mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:focal)
Phases (As planned)
Core Brain (Lambda + basic PHDM + Playwright)
Agent Capabilities (Vision + Action validator + Spiralverse)
Full Tests (Swarm + stress + Lyapunov stability)
8. Key Innovations & Differentiation
<table>
9. Hyperbolic Vector Encryption (H-LWE)
Method
Hyperbolic LWE (H-LWE) - lightweight symmetric/asymmetric hybrid for Poincaré vectors:
Map vectors to hyperbolic lattice points via exp_map
Add noise in hyperbolic distance (not Euclidean)
Decryption uses log_map + alignment
Key Properties
Decrypted vectors remain inside safe radius (r < 0.95)
Operations respect hyperbolic metrics (distances, Möbius additions)
Invalid/attacked ciphertexts naturally drift toward boundary
Superior to flat encryption: preserves geometric hierarchy
Integration
Symmetric variant: Fast for intra-agent memory rings
Asymmetric variant: Spiralverse mesh using QuasiLWE phason keys
Containment guarantee: Decryption auto-rejects if radius drifts (detects tampering)
Complete System Summary
This is the complete, consolidated SCBE-AETHERMOORE system—grounded in geometry, safe by design, and extendable into novel cryptographic primitives. The framework provides:
✅ Provable safety through geometric containment
✅ Hallucination resistance via quasicrystal topology
✅ Post-quantum security with novel primitives
✅ Multi-agent coordination with Byzantine fault tolerance
✅ Unified vision-reasoning-action manifold
✅ Production-ready infrastructure (AWS Lambda, GCP, Docker)
Version: 3.0.0 (consolidated architecture)
Maintained by: Issac Davis
Status: Active development with production deployment phases
